perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Approved Location. The street address of the location for the Franchised Business approved under this Agreement is specified in Exhibit A to this Agreement, and is referred to as the "Approved Location."
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Approved Location. The street address of the location for the Franchised Business approved under this Agreement is specified in Exhibit A to this Agreement, and is referred to as the "Designated Territory."
perturbation.explanation: The change of "Approved Location" to "Designated Territory" introduces misaligned terminology. Throughout the document, the original term 'Approved Location' is used to define the geographical limit where the franchise can operate. By redefining it here as 'Designated Territory,' we imply a different type of right—a protected area, which directly contradicts Section 1.3 which states 'No Protected Territory'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 No Protected Territory. You expressly acknowledge and agree that this franchise is non-exclusive, and that this Agreement does not grant or imply any protected area or territory for the Franchised Business. Accordingly, we retain the right to conduct any business and sell Page 2 of 80 services and products at any location, notwithstanding the proximity of that business activity to the Approved Location. We retain all rights, including but not limited to: (a) the right to use, and to license others to use, the System and the Proprietary Marks for the operation of Goosehead Businesses at any location; (b) the right to sell, and to license others to sell, products and services (including Services) that are also authorized for sale at Goosehead Businesses through other channels of distribution (including, but not limited to, through catalogs, mail order, toll free numbers, sales via Internet websites, and other forms of electronic commerce); (c) the right to acquire and operate businesses of any kind and to grant or franchise the right to others to operate other businesses of any kind, no matter where located; and (d) the right to use and license the use of the Proprietary Marks and other marks in connection with the operation of businesses at any location, which businesses and marks may be the same as, similar to, or different from the Franchised Business and the Proprietary Marks, on such terms and conditions as we deem advisable, and without granting you any rights therein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Approved Location" as "Designated Territory," which directly conflicts with section 1.3, 'No Protected Territory.' The original term, "Approved Location," simply defines the physical site, while "Designated Territory" implies a protected area. This implication directly contradicts the explicit statement that no protected territory exists, creating a strong in-text contradiction that affects the franchise's operating rights and could lead to legal disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Royalty Fee. We will receive all Commissions (defined below) from insurance carriers. We will receive all Premiums (defined below), Policy Fees (defined below) and Agency Fees on your behalf. If the event that any Premiums, Policy Fees, or Agency Fees are received directly by you, these funds must be forwarded to us within twenty four (24) hours of receipt. We will retain Agency Fees and will forward Premiums and Policy Fees to the insurance carriers. Beginning on the date you begin operations under this Agreement, which is agreed to be the date set out in the Declarations Page (the "Commencement Date"), we will remit to you Net Revenues on a monthly basis. As used in this Agreement:
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Royalty Fee. We will receive all Commissions (defined below) from insurance carriers. We will receive all Premiums (defined below), Policy Fees (defined below) and Agency Fees on your behalf. If the event that any Premiums, Policy Fees, or Agency Fees are received directly by you, these funds must be forwarded to us within twenty four (24) hours of receipt. We will retain the Service Charge and will forward Premiums and Policy Fees to the insurance carriers. Beginning on the date you begin operations under this Agreement, which is agreed to be the date set out in the Declarations Page (the "Commencement Date"), we will remit to you Net Revenues on a monthly basis. As used in this Agreement:
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Agency Fees" to "Service Charge" in the context of retention, while still defining "Agency Fees" later in the section, creates a conflict. The contract defines "Agency Fees", but indicates retaining "Service Charge" this creates ambiguity regarding what is being retained.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2.1 the term "Agency Fees" will mean fees that are charged by you for issuing a new policy pursuant to the Manual.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'Agency Fees' to 'Service Charge' in the context of retention. However, Section 4.2.1 defines 'Agency Fees'. This creates a contradiction because the contract now indicates retaining 'Service Charge' while still defining and referencing 'Agency Fees', leading to ambiguity about what fees are being retained. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 15.1 Required Insurance Coverage. Before starting any activities or operations under this Agreement, you agree to procure and maintain in full force and effect during the term of this Agreement (and for such period thereafter as is necessary to provide the coverages required under this Agreement for events having occurred during the Term of this Agreement), at your expense, at least the following insurance policy or policies in connection with the Franchised Business or other facilities on premises, or by reason of the construction, operation, or occupancy of the Franchised Business or other facilities on premises. Such policy or policies must be written by an insurance company or companies we have approved, having at all times a rating of at least "A-" in the most recent Key Rating Guide published by the A.M. Best Company (or another rating that we reasonably designate if A.M. Best Company no longer Page 35 of 80 publishes the Key Rating Guide) and licensed and admitted to do business in the state in which the Franchised Business is located, and must include, at a minimum (except that we may reasonably specify additional coverages and higher policy limits for all franchisees periodically in the Manual or otherwise in writing to reflect inflation, identification of new risks, changes in the law or standards of liability, higher damage awards and other relevant changes in circumstances), the following:
perturbation.changed_text: 15.1 Required Insurance Coverage. Before starting any activities or operations under this Agreement, you agree to procure and maintain in full force and effect during the term of this Agreement (and for such period thereafter as is necessary to provide the coverages required under this Agreement for events having occurred during the Term of this Agreement), at your expense, at least the following insurance policy or policies in connection with the Franchised Business or other facilities on premises, or by reason of the construction, operation, or occupancy of the Franchised Business or other facilities on premises. Such policy or policies must be written by an insurance company or companies we have approved, having at all times a rating of at least "A-" in the most recent Key Rating Guide published by the A.M. Best Company (or another rating that we reasonably designate if A.M. Best Company no longer Page 35 of 80 publishes the Key Rating Guide) and licensed and admitted to do business in the state in which the Approved Location is situated, and must include, at a minimum (except that we may reasonably specify additional coverages and higher policy limits for all franchisees periodically in the Manual or otherwise in writing to reflect inflation, identification of new risks, changes in the law or standards of liability, higher damage awards and other relevant changes in circumstances), the following:
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'state in which the Franchised Business is located' to 'state in which the Approved Location is situated' can create contradiction and uncertainty. While seemingly similar, if the Franchised Business operates across state lines (even if the Approved Location is in one state), it could lead to disputes about which state's insurance regulations apply. Section 1.4.2 indicates that Services delivered must be to customers located in the state the Approved Location is situated, but the insurance coverages must be valid in all areas of operation. This can be especially problematic for policies that may have interstate implications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4.2 You agree not to offer or sell any services or products (including the Services and Products) through any means other than through the Franchised Business as provided in this Section 1.4; and therefore, for example, you agree not to offer or sell services or products from satellite locations, temporary locations, mobile vehicles or formats, carts or kiosks. Unless you become licensed in another state and receive prior written approval from us to offer insurance policies in that state, you may only provide and deliver Services to customers located within the State in which the Approved Location is situated.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'state in which the Franchised Business is located' to 'state in which the Approved Location is situated' in the insurance requirements. This creates a contradiction with section 1.4.2, which states that services can only be delivered to customers located within the state the Approved Location is situated. However, insurance coverage as required by section 15.1 must be valid in all areas of operation, which could extend beyond the state of the Approved Location, creating a contradiction between the geographic scope of operations and the state of insurance coverage, making the terms inconsistent.
